title.value: Electroreductive Carboxylation of Propargylic Acetates with CO2: Access to Tetrasubstituted 2,3-Allenoates

abstract.value: An electroreductive carboxylation of propargylic alcohols with CO2 and then workup with TMSCHN2 to construct tetrasubstituted 2,3-allenoates is developed. This method allows the incorporation of an external ester group into the resulting allene system through electroreduction, carboxylation, and deacetoxylation cascades. Mechanistically, electricity on/off experiments and cyclic voltammetry analysis support the preferential generation of radical anion intermediates based on the electronic nature of the aryl rings.

warnings: The abstract text appears to contain OCR inconsistencies, including use of 'propargylic alcohols' versus the title/scope's 'propargylic acetates'.

The visible abstract says 'propargylic alcohols', but the title, scope tables, and experimental discussion consistently indicate propargylic acetates; substrate classification follows the latter evidence.
The paper centers on electroreductive, transition-metal-free conditions, but the controlled vocabulary does not provide an electrochemistry label, so catalyst_or_method is set to 'not specified'.

value.reaction_type: carbonylative synthesis of allenes and allenoates
